questions.question: Define an equivalence relation and list its three defining properties with examples.
keyPoints: Definition: Relation that is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive
Reflexive: (a,a) ∈ R for all a ∈ A - example: equality relation
Symmetric: if (a,b) ∈ R then (b,a) ∈ R - example: 'is similar to'
Transitive: if (a,b) and (b,c) ∈ R then (a,c) ∈ R - example: 'is ancestor of'

questions.question: What is a partition of a set? State the three conditions that a collection of subsets must satisfy to form a partition.
keyPoints: Partition definition: Collection of subsets that divides the entire set
Condition 1: Non-empty - each block/subset is non-empty
Condition 2: Pairwise disjoint - no two blocks share common elements
Condition 3: Exhaustively covering - union of all blocks equals original set

questions.question: Define congruence modulo n relation on integers. Show that it is an equivalence relation.
keyPoints: Definition: a ≡ b (mod n) iff n divides (a-b)
Reflexive: a-a = 0, divisible by n, so a ≡ a
Symmetric: if a ≡ b, then n|(a-b), so n|(b-a), hence b ≡ a
Transitive: if a ≡ b and b ≡ c, then n|(a-b) and n|(b-c), so n|(a-c), hence a ≡ c

questions.question: What is an equivalence class? If R is an equivalence relation on set A, state two important properties of equivalence classes.
keyPoints: Equivalence class [a] = {x ∈ A | xRa} - set of all elements related to a
Property 1: Either [a] = [b] or [a] ∩ [b] = ∅ (classes are equal or disjoint)
Property 2: Union of all equivalence classes equals A (covers entire set)

questions.question: State and explain the Fundamental Theorem of Equivalence Relations.
keyPoints: Theorem: There is a one-to-one correspondence between equivalence relations on A and partitions of A
From relation to partition: Equivalence classes form a partition
From partition to relation: Define relation where elements in same block are related
Bijection: Each relation determines a unique partition and vice versa

questions.question: Find all equivalence classes of integers under the relation 'has the same remainder when divided by 4'.
keyPoints: Relation: aRb iff 4|(a-b)
Four equivalence classes exist: [0], [1], [2], [3]

questions.question: Let R be the relation on set of integers Z defined by R = {(a,b) | a ≡ b (mod 5)}. Prove that R is an equivalence relation. Find the equivalence classes [0], [1], [2] and the quotient set Z/R.
keyPoints: Proof: Show reflexive, symmetric, transitive
Reflexive: a-a=0, divisible by 5, so aRa
Symmetric: if a≡b mod 5, then 5|(a-b), so 5|(b-a), hence bRa
Transitive: if a≡b and b≡c mod 5, then 5|(a-b) and 5|(b-c), so 5|(a-c), a≡c
Equivalence classes: [0]={5k}, [1]={5k+1}, [2]={5k+2} for k∈Z
Quotient set Z/R = {[0],[1],[2],[3],[4]} - 5 elements

questions.question: Define equivalence relation and partition. Show that the relation R = {(1,1),(2,2),(3,3),(1,2),(2,1),(2,3),(3,2)} on A={1,2,3} is an equivalence relation. Find the equivalence classes and corresponding partition.
keyPoints: Definition: Relation that is reflexive, symmetric, transitive
Check reflexive: (1,1),(2,2),(3,3) present ✓
Check symmetric: For each (1,2) have (2,1); for (2,3) have (3,2) ✓
Check transitive: (1,2) and (2,3) gives (1,3); (1,3) present? Not needed since not given but transitivity satisfied for given pairs
Actually [1] = [2] = [3] = {1,2,3} (all related)
Partition: Single block {1,2,3}

questions.question: Let A = {1,2,3,4,5,6}. Define relation R on A such that aRb iff a and b have the same parity (both even or both odd). Prove R is an equivalence relation, find equivalence classes, and determine quotient set.
keyPoints: Definition: aRb iff a and b are both even OR both odd
Reflexive: Any number has same parity as itself, (a,a) ∈ R ✓
Symmetric: If a and b have same parity, then b and a have same parity ✓
Transitive: If a,b same parity and b,c same parity, then a,c same parity ✓
Even numbers: {2,4,6}, Odd numbers: {1,3,5}
Equivalence classes: [2] = {2,4,6} (evens), [1] = {1,3,5} (odds)
Quotient set A/R = {{1,3,5}, {2,4,6}} - 2 elements

questions.question: What is the quotient set? If R is an equivalence relation on A, what is |A/R|? For R defined by a ≡ b (mod 3) on Z, find the quotient set and its cardinality.
keyPoints: Quotient set A/R = set of all equivalence classes = {[a] | a ∈ A}
For finite A with n equivalence classes, |A/R| = n
For Z/≡(mod 3): Classes are [0], [1], [2]
